_help_about: Keys are GM MIDI drum pitches as decimal strings (27..87 is the
Values are LOTRO ABC drum-slot tokens (C, c, c', ^D, etc.).
The --drum-map flag merges these entries onto the built-in
defaults; unlisted pitches keep their defaults. Keys that start
with '_' are treated as documentation and ignored by the loader.
Reference data lifted from Maestro's legacy drummaps
_help_gm_drum_names: GM drum names (what a MIDI pitch REPRESENTS):
33 Metronome Click     58 Vibraslap           76 High Wood Block
34 Metronome Bell      59 Ride Cymbal 2       77 Low Wood Block
35 Acoustic Bass Drum  60 Hi Bongo            78 Mute Cuica
36 Bass Drum 1         61 Low Bongo           79 Open Cuica
37 Side Stick          62 Mute Hi Conga       80 Mute Triangle
38 Acoustic Snare      63 Open Hi Conga       81 Open Triangle
40 Electric Snare      65 High Timbale        83 Jingle Bell
41 Low Floor Tom       66 Low Timbale         84 Bell Tree
43 High Floor Tom      68 Low Agogo           86 Mute Surdo
_help_lotro_slots: LOTRO drum-slot sounds (what an ABC letter PLAYS):
C,  = Conga High 1     C   = Muted Mid        c   = Rattle 3
D,  = Bongo High       D   = Bass Slap 2      d   = Bend Low Up
E,  = Slap 2           E   = Slap 3           e   = Bend Mid Down
F,  = Muted 1          F   = Rim Shot 2       f   = Bend High Up
G,  = Tom High 1       G   = Rattle 1         g   = Conga Low
A,  = Tom High 2       A   = Rattle Long      a   = Bongo Low
B,  = Tom Mid 1        B   = Rattle 2         b   = Conga Mid
Slot names are from Maestro's drummaps/default.txt. When you write
'38' -> 'F' below, you are saying: a General-MIDI snare hit should
trigger LOTRO's 'Muted 1' slot. Pick a slot that SOUNDS right for
_help_defaults: The entries below reproduce the built-in defaults from spec §2.6
plus extended GM percussion (52-59 folded onto closest-kin cymbal
and ride slots). If you only want to override a few drums, delete
the ones you're happy with — they stay at these defaults.
